prompt_text: Best way to manage preview deployments for every pull request for a frontend engineering teams at SaaS companies team
response:
# Preview Deployments for Frontend Teams

## Architecture Overview

```
PR Created
    ↓
GitHub/GitLab Actions triggered
    ↓
Build artifact + Deploy to preview environment
    ↓
Generate preview URL
    ↓
Post URL to PR
    ↓
Review → Merge → Cleanup
```

## Implementation Approaches

### 1. **Vercel (Recommended for Most Teams)**

**Pros:**
- Zero-config deployment
- Automatic previews on every PR
- Production-grade performance
- Built-in analytics & monitoring
- Preview comments on PRs

**Setup:**
```yaml
# Nearly automatic with GitHub/GitLab integration
# Just connect repo → automatic previews
```

**Cost considerations:** ~$20-100/month depending on usage

---

### 2. **Self-Hosted: AWS + CloudFront**

**Architecture:**
```
S3 (artifact storage)
    ↓
CloudFront (CDN)
    ↓
Route53 (DNS for pr-{number}.preview.yourapp.com)
```

**GitHub Actions workflow:**
```yaml
name: Preview Deployment

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Build
        run: npm run build
      
      - name: Deploy to S3
        uses: jakejarvis/s3-sync-action@master
        with:
          args: --delete
        env:
          AWS_S3_BUCKET: ${{ secrets.PREVIEW_BUCKET }}
          AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          AWS_REGION: 'us-east-1'
          SOURCE_DIR: 'dist'
          DEST_DIR: 'pr-${{ github.event.number }}'
      
      - name: Comment PR with URL
        uses: actions/github-script@v6
        with:
          script: |
            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: `🚀 Preview: https://preview.yourapp.com/pr-${context.issue.number}`
            })
      
      - name: Cleanup on close
        if: github.event.action == 'closed'
        run: |
          aws s3 rm s3://${{ secrets.PREVIEW_BUCKET }}/pr-${{ github.event.number }}/ --recursive
```

---

### 3. **Netlify**

**Simple setup:**
```yaml
name: Netlify Preview

on: [pull_request]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      - run: npm ci && npm run build
      - uses: nwtgck/actions-netlify@v2
        with:
          publish-dir: './dist'
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          deploy-message: "Preview for PR #${{ github.event.number }}"
```

---

### 4. **Container-Based: Docker + Registry**

For teams needing custom backends:

```dockerfile
# Dockerfile
FROM node:18-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["npm", "start"]
```

```yaml
name: Preview Docker

on: [pull_request]

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v3
      
      - name: Build & Push Docker Image
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v4
        with:
          push: true
          tags: registry.example.com/app:pr-${{ github.event.number }}
          registry: ghcr.io
      
      - name: Deploy to K8s/ECS
        run: |
          # kubectl apply
